intent: Fix imported course lesson ordering by extracting shared toSortedVideos() helper with path-based deterministic sort, applying it to scanCourseFolder and scanCourseFolderFromHandle. Also fix Authors page: count imported courses via totalCoursesForAuthor()/withAuthorCourseCounts(), sanitize specialty badges with flattenSpecialties(), and fix card layout overflow.

findings.title: Integration tests don't assert sort order after import
findings.why_it_matters: toSortedVideos() is a new module-private helper that determines import ordering. Integration tests in courseImport.test.ts call the full import pipeline but only assert videoCount, not that videos are in path-sorted order. A sort regression wouldn't be caught. Since the function is private, add sort-order assertions to existing integration tests instead of writing separate unit tests.
evidence: toSortedVideos() at src/lib/courseImport.ts:82-104 is module-private (not exported)
courseImport.test.ts tests importCourseFromFolder but only asserts videoCount (line 103)
A sort regression would pass all existing tests
findings.suggested_fix: Add order assertion to integration test: after importing a course with videos at paths '01-intro/1.mp4' and '02-advanced/1.mp4', assert stored videos have correct sequential order values.

findings.title: flattenSpecialties splits legitimate comma-containing specialty names
findings.why_it_matters: flattenSpecialties splits on comma/semicolon/pipe. A specialty like 'Systems Design, Architecture & Planning' would be incorrectly split into two entries. Users importing author data with comma-containing specialties will see them fragmented. No escape mechanism exists for literal commas.
evidence: Regex /[,;|]/ at src/lib/authors.ts:41 splits on comma
Test confirms split behavior: ['A, B, C'] -> ['A', 'B', 'C']
No quoting or escaping mechanism in the function

details.R1: met — toSortedVideos sorts by path with localeCompare numeric:true
details.R3: met — scanFromDroppedFiles already sorts by file.name (lines 797-800, pre-existing)
details.R4: met — getFirstLessonId uses sortBy('order')/sortBy('filename'); getLastWatchedLesson has tiebreaker (line 130)
details.R5: met — useCourseAdapter.ts uses sortBy(); useNextBestCourse.ts uses sortBy(); progress.ts has tiebreaker (all pre-existing)
details.R6: met — no schema migration, existing courses keep current order

implementation_units.Unit 1: implemented in diff — toSortedVideos applied to scanCourseFolderFromHandle
implementation_units.Unit 5: pre-existing — getFirstLessonId already uses sortBy('filename') for PDFs

residual_risks: Existing courses imported before this fix retain scrambled order values — users must re-import or use VideoReorderDialog
learnings: Plan references docs/solutions/ui-bugs/lesson-badge-local-global-index-mismatch-2026-05-04.md — sortBy() pattern for ordering
testing_gaps: Integration tests don't assert sort order after import
